key_insight: SinAE uses one Transformer-based latent autoencoder plus flow-matching decoding to unify generation across molecules, crystals, and proteins without domain-specific geometry operators.
clinical_relevance: By enabling shared atomic representations across domains, this approach could accelerate early-stage therapeutic discovery by improving generative modeling for small molecules and protein-related design tasks.
method: Vanilla Transformer encoder/decoder for all three atomic domains
Flow-matching decoder reconstructs 3D structures from compact latents
Shared latent prior is trained with a Diffusion Transformer for generation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports near-lossless reconstruction and orders-of-magnitude lower reconstruction error than prior latent baselines, plus improved molecule–crystal joint training performance.
watch_item: Results are based on benchmark reconstruction and generation tasks, so real-world utility still depends on robustness, scaling, and validation in downstream discovery workflows.
paper_title: SinAE: A Single-Architecture Flow-Matching Autoencoder for Cross-Domain Atomic Systems
